title: NBA Daily Digest
subtitle: March 18, 2026: Expansion Talks Heat Up, Stars Shine on the Hardwood!

items.headline: NBA Board of Governors to Vote on Expansion to Seattle and Las Vegas Next Week!
items.summary: The NBA may move a step closer to adding teams in Seattle and Las Vegas next week, with the league's board of governors set to discuss and potentially vote on the proposal. A three-fourths majority (23 of 30 teams) would be required to approve the motion. Owners are also expected to receive an update on plans for a new league in Europe.

items.headline: Cunningham Sustains Back Spasms; Star Players Out for 76ers, Pacers, and Clippers
items.summary: Detroit's All-Star Cade Cunningham exited last night's game with back spasms. The Philadelphia 76ers were severely short-handed in their loss to Denver, missing Joel Embiid (right oblique strain), Tyrese Maxey (sprained finger), and Kelly Oubre Jr. (left elbow strain), while Paul George is serving a 25-game suspension. The Indiana Pacers will be without Tyrese Haliburton and Johnny Furphy for the rest of the season due to Achilles and knee injuries, respectively. For tonight's Clippers game, Kawhi Leonard (ankle) and Darius Garland (toe) are questionable, and Bradley Beal is out for the season with a hip injury. Damian Lillard (Achilles) and Shaedon Sharpe (calf) are out for the season for the Trail Blazers.

items.headline: Trail Blazers Coast Past Nets
items.summary: Portland started their road trip strong, never trailing against a short-handed Brooklyn squad.

items.headline: Duren Dominates as Pistons Beat Wizards
items.summary: Despite losing Cade Cunningham to injury, the Pistons secured a comfortable victory.

items.headline: Knicks Extend Win Streak, Pacers' Woes Continue
items.summary: Josh Hart exploded for a season-high as the Knicks handed the Pacers their 14th straight loss.

items.headline: Nuggets Rout Short-Handed 76ers
items.summary: Denver cruised to a dominant win over an injury-plagued Philadelphia team, keeping pace in the West.

items.headline: Spurs Blow Out Kings Behind Wembanyama
items.summary: San Antonio continued its strong run with a commanding performance on the road.

items.headline: Hawks Soar Past Magic
items.summary: Atlanta extended their impressive winning streak with a strong offensive outing.

items.headline: Bulls Snap Losing Streak Against Grizzlies
items.summary: Chicago got back in the win column with a decisive victory over Memphis.

items.headline: Celtics Edge Suns in Tight Contest
items.summary: Boston held off a strong performance from Devin Booker to secure a narrow win.

items.headline: Spurs Top Clippers in Close Battle
items.summary: San Antonio earned another victory, showing their strength in a tight matchup.

items.headline: Jalen Duren's Dominant Double-Double
items.statContext: Duren led the Eastern Conference-leading Pistons to a victory over the Wizards.

items.headline: Josh Hart's Scoring Explosion
items.statContext: Hart had a season-high performance, shooting lights out from the field and beyond the arc against the Pacers.

items.headline: Nikil Alexander-Walker Erupts for 41
items.statContext: Alexander-Walker was the leading scorer in the Hawks' win over the Magic.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's All-Around Impact
items.statContext: The Israeli All-Star forward showcased his versatility, leading the Trail Blazers to a win over the Nets.

items.headline: Maxime Raynaud's Career Night
items.statContext: The rookie put up a season-high in points and rebounds against the Spurs.

items.headline: Ben Saraf's Strong Rookie Showing
items.statContext: The Israeli rookie delivered his best performance off the bench, sparking a mini-comeback for the Nets.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic's Playmaking Masterclass
items.statContext: Jokic orchestrated the Nuggets' offense with ease in their dominant win over the 76ers.

items.headline: Russell Westbrook Climbs All-Time Assists List
items.statContext: Westbrook moved past Mark Jackson and Steve Nash into fifth place on the NBA's all-time assists list.

items.headline: Jaylen Johnson's Triple-Double
items.statContext: Johnson delivered a triple-double performance in Atlanta's victory.

items.headline: Josh Giddey Joins Triple-Double Club
items.statContext: Giddey recorded a triple-double in the Bulls' win over the Grizzlies.

items.headline: Western Conference Playoff Picture Tightens Up
items.summary: The Western Conference playoff race remains incredibly tight, with just 1.5 games separating the third-place Los Angeles Lakers and the sixth-place Minnesota Timberwolves. Every game down the stretch will be crucial for seeding.

items.headline: San Antonio Spurs on a Roll
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs continue their impressive run, having won 8 of their last 9 games and boasting a league-best 19-2 record since February 1st.

items.headline: Indiana Pacers Facing Steep Decline
items.summary: The Indiana Pacers are in a difficult stretch, extending their losing streak to 14 consecutive games.
